actions.name: ASK_USER_QUESTION
actions.description: Broadcast 1-4 structured questions back to the user. Each question has a short header, a full question string, and optional multi-choice options with descriptions and previews. This is a structured-question broadcast surface — the action returns the question payload as data so a UI layer can render it; the action does NOT block waiting for an answer. UI integration is pending; for now treat the response as a published question, not as an interactive prompt.

parameters.name: questions
parameters.description: Array of 1-4 question objects. Each: { question: string, header: string, options?: Array<{label, description?, preview?}>, multiSelect?: boolean }. If options is empty/undefined, the question is treated as freeform.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Broadcast 1-4 structured questions to the user (UI integration pending; non-blocking).

exampleCalls.user: Use ASK_USER_QUESTION with the provided parameters.

actions.name: ATTACK_NPC
actions.description: Engage a nearby NPC in combat by its instance id. The server pathfinds the agent into attack range automatically.

parameters.name: npcId
parameters.description: Nearby NPC instance id from the SCAPE_NEARBY provider.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Attack NPC by id.

exampleCalls.user: Use ATTACK_NPC with the provided parameters.

actions.name: BASH
actions.description: Execute a shell command via /bin/bash -c <command>. Runs in the session cwd by default. Foreground commands return stdout, stderr, and exit code. Long-running commands auto-promote to background and return a task_id; pass run_in_background=true to background immediately. Paths under the configured blocklist (e.g. ~/pvt, ~/Library, ~/.ssh) are off-limits as cwd.

parameters.name: command
parameters.description: Shell command to run; executed via /bin/bash -c <command>.

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: Five to ten word humanly-readable summary of the command.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Hard timeout in ms; clamped to [100, 600000]. Default 120000.

parameters.name: cwd
parameters.description: Absolute working directory; must not resolve under a blocked path. Defaults to the session cwd.

parameters.name: run_in_background
parameters.description: If true, return a task_id immediately. Use TASK_OUTPUT to poll and TASK_STOP to terminate.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Run a shell command (foreground or background).

exampleCalls.user: Use BASH with the provided parameters.

actions.name: BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE
actions.description: Block websites until a specific todo is marked complete. Use this only when the unblock condition is finishing a task, workout, assignment, or todo, like 'block x.com until I finish my workout'.

parameters.name: websites
parameters.description: List of website hostnames to block.

parameters.name: todoId
parameters.description: ID of an existing todo. Preferred over todoName when known.

parameters.name: todoName
parameters.description: Name of the todo. Resolved against active todos; created if no match.

parameters.name: unlockDurationMinutes
parameters.description: Optional: once the gate is satisfied, re-lock the same websites after this many minutes.

parameters.name: profile
parameters.description: Optional profile label for the block rule.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Block websites until a named todo is completed.

exampleCalls.user: Use BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE with the provided parameters.

parameters.name: text

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: BlueBubbles chat guid, handle, or current conversation.

parameters.name: messageGuid

actions.descriptionCompressed: Bluebubbles message ops: send, react.

actions.name: BROWSER_ACTION
actions.description:
browser_action:
  purpose: Control a Chromium-based browser through the local runtime: launch, navigate, interact, inspect, execute JavaScript, wait, and manage tabs.
  provider_state: Read-only browser availability and recent action state are available from the computerState provider. Use state/info/list_tabs only for explicit live refreshes.
  flow: Open or connect first, then navigate and interact. Use clickables to discover interactive elements.
  actions: open/connect/close/navigate/click/type/scroll/screenshot/dom/get_dom/clickables/get_clickables/execute/state/info/context/get_context/wait/list_tabs/open_tab/close_tab/switch_tab.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Browser action to perform.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: URL for open, navigate, or open_tab.

parameters.name: selector
parameters.description: CSS selector for click, type, or wait.

parameters.name: coordinate
parameters.description: Viewport [x, y] coordinate for click.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: Text to type, text to click, or text to wait for.

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: JavaScript source to execute in the page.

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: Scroll direction.

parameters.name: amount
parameters.description: Scroll amount in pixels.

parameters.name: tabId
parameters.description: Tab identifier for tab actions.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Timeout in milliseconds for wait actions.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Chromium browser control router: open/connect/navigate/click/type/read dom/clickables/execute/wait/tabs; read-only state.

exampleCalls.user: Use BROWSER_ACTION with the provided parameters.

actions.name: CLOUD_AGENT
actions.description: ElizaCloud agent ops router: provision a container, freeze (snapshot+stop) a running agent, resume a frozen agent from snapshot, or check credit balance and runtime estimate.

parameters.name: op
parameters.description: Which cloud-agent operation to run: 'provision', 'freeze', 'resume', or 'check_credits'.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Human-readable agent name. Required for op='provision' and op='resume'.

parameters.name: project_name
parameters.description: Project identifier (lowercase, no spaces). Required for op='provision' and op='resume'.

parameters.name: containerId
parameters.description: Container ID. Required for op='freeze'.

parameters.name: snapshotId
parameters.description: Specific snapshot ID for op='resume' (defaults to latest).

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: Optional description for op='provision'.

parameters.name: environment_vars
parameters.description: Additional environment variables for op='provision' or op='resume'.

parameters.name: auto_backup
parameters.description: Enable periodic auto-backup for op='provision' (default: true).

parameters.name: detailed
parameters.description: Include transaction history for op='check_credits'.

parameters.name: confirmed
parameters.description: Must be true to execute mutating ops ('provision', 'freeze', 'resume') after the preview.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Cloud agent ops: provision, freeze, resume, check credits.

exampleCalls.user: Use CLOUD_AGENT with the provided parameters.

actions.name: COMMAND
actions.description: Slash-command router. Operations: help, status, stop, models, list. Selects the operation from parameters.op or the detected /<command> in the message text.

parameters.name: op
parameters.description: Command operation. One of: help, status, stop, models, list.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Slash commands: help, status, stop, models, list.

exampleCalls.user: Use COMMAND with the provided parameters.

actions.name: COMPUTER_USE
actions.description:
computer_use:
  purpose: Canonical cross-platform computer-use action for real desktop interaction on macOS, Linux, and Windows when direct computer operation is required.
  guidance: Take a screenshot before acting. After each desktop action, the result includes a screenshot when available. Use this standard plugin action, not a LifeOps wrapper, for Finder/Desktop/native-app/browser/file/terminal workflows on the owner's machine.
  actions: screenshot/click/click_with_modifiers/double_click/right_click/mouse_move/type/key/key_combo/scroll/drag/detect_elements/ocr.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Desktop action to perform.

parameters.name: coordinate
parameters.description: Target [x, y] pixel coordinate.

parameters.name: startCoordinate
parameters.description: Start [x, y] pixel coordinate for drag.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: Text to type.

parameters.name: modifiers
parameters.description: Modifier keys to hold during click_with_modifiers, e.g. ['cmd', 'shift'] or ['ctrl'].

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: Single key or combo string depending on action.

parameters.name: button
parameters.description: Mouse button for click_with_modifiers.

parameters.name: clicks
parameters.description: Number of clicks for click_with_modifiers.

parameters.name: scrollDirection
parameters.description: Scroll direction.

parameters.name: scrollAmount
parameters.description: Scroll tick count.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Canonical cross-platform desktop control: screenshot/click/modified click/double/right/move/type/key/key_combo/scroll/drag/detect_elements/ocr.

exampleCalls.user: Use COMPUTER_USE with the provided parameters.

actions.name: EDIT
actions.description: Replace text in an existing file. Default behavior requires `old_string` to match exactly once; pass `replace_all=true` to substitute every occurrence. The file must have been READ in this session, must still match its recorded mtime, and the new content cannot introduce a detected secret pattern.

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.description: Absolute path to the file to edit.

parameters.name: old_string
parameters.description: Exact substring to replace.

parameters.name: new_string
parameters.description: Replacement text. Must differ from old_string.

parameters.name: replace_all
parameters.description: If true, replace every occurrence; otherwise require exactly one match.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Replace exact-match text in a file (single match by default; pass replace_all for multiple).

exampleCalls.user: Use EDIT with the provided parameters.

actions.name: ENTER_WORKTREE
actions.description: Create a git worktree for the current repo and switch the session into it. The new worktree path becomes the session cwd and a sandbox root, so subsequent file operations land there until EXIT_WORKTREE pops it. Use to isolate a parallel branch of work without disturbing the main checkout.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Optional worktree branch/dir name. Defaults to a random auto-* identifier.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Optional absolute worktree directory. Must lie within sandbox roots. Defaults to a per-call directory under the OS temp dir.

parameters.name: base
parameters.description: Optional base ref for the new worktree (default HEAD).

actions.descriptionCompressed: Create and switch into a git worktree for parallel work.

exampleCalls.user: Use ENTER_WORKTREE with the provided parameters.

actions.name: EXECUTE_TRADE
actions.description: Execute a BSC token trade (buy or sell). Use this when a user asks to

parameters.name: side
parameters.description: Trade direction: "buy" or "sell"

parameters.name: tokenAddress
parameters.description: BSC token contract address (0x-prefixed, 40 hex characters)

parameters.name: amount
parameters.description: Human-readable trade amount (e.g. "0.5" BNB for buys, or token amount for sells)

parameters.name: slippageBps
parameters.description: Slippage tolerance in basis points (default 300 = 3%)

parameters.name: routeProvider
parameters.description: Route provider preference for the swap: "pancakeswap-v2" or "0x". Defaults to "pancakeswap-v2".

actions.descriptionCompressed: Execute BSC token trade (buy/sell) via PancakeSwap (admin/owner only).

exampleCalls.user: Use EXECUTE_TRADE with the provided parameters.

actions.name: EXIT_WORKTREE
actions.description: Pop the most recent ENTER_WORKTREE: restore the previous session cwd, drop the added sandbox root, and (with cleanup=true) run `git worktree remove --force` to delete the worktree directory.

parameters.name: cleanup
parameters.description: If true, also `git worktree remove --force` the popped worktree directory.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Exit current worktree, restore previous cwd, optionally git worktree remove --force.

exampleCalls.user: Use EXIT_WORKTREE with the provided parameters.

actions.name: GLOB
actions.description: Find files matching a glob pattern (e.g. '**/*.ts'). Returns up to 100 absolute paths sorted by mtime descending. Excludes VCS, build, and dependency directories. Use this instead of BASH for file discovery.

parameters.name: pattern
parameters.description: Glob pattern relative to the search root (e.g. '**/*.ts').

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Absolute path of the directory to search. Defaults to the session cwd.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Find files by glob (e.g. '**/*.ts'); returns absolute paths sorted by mtime.

exampleCalls.user: Use GLOB with the provided parameters.

actions.name: GREP
actions.description: Search file contents using ripgrep (a fast regex search). Returns matching files, counts, or line content. Always excludes VCS directories. Use this instead of BASH for content search.

parameters.name: pattern
parameters.description: Regex pattern to search for.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Absolute path to a file or directory to search. Defaults to the session cwd.

parameters.name: glob
parameters.description: Optional glob filter passed to ripgrep -g (e.g. '*.ts').

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Optional ripgrep file type passed via -t (e.g. 'js', 'py').

parameters.name: output_mode
parameters.description: How to render matches: 'content' returns matching lines, 'files_with_matches' returns file paths, 'count' returns per-file counts. Defaults to 'files_with_matches'.

parameters.name: -A
parameters.description: Lines of context to show after each match (content mode).

parameters.name: -B
parameters.description: Lines of context to show before each match (content mode).

parameters.name: -C
parameters.description: Lines of context around each match (content mode).

parameters.name: case_insensitive
parameters.description: Match case-insensitively (alias of -i).

parameters.name: multiline
parameters.description: Enable multiline matching (the pattern can span newlines).

parameters.name: head_limit
parameters.description: Truncate output to the first N lines. 0 means unlimited. Defaults to CODING_TOOLS_GREP_HEAD_LIMIT or 250.

parameters.name: show_line_numbers
parameters.description: Show 1-based line numbers in content mode.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Ripgrep wrapper: regex search across files, returns matches/counts/files.

exampleCalls.user: Use GREP with the provided parameters.

actions.name: LINEAR
actions.description: Manage Linear issues, comments, and activity. Operations: create_issue, get_issue, update_issue, delete_issue, create_comment, update_comment, delete_comment, list_comments, get_activity, clear_activity, search_issues. The op is inferred from the message text when not explicitly provided.

parameters.name: op
parameters.description: Operation to perform. One of: create_issue, get_issue, update_issue, delete_issue, create_comment, update_comment, delete_comment, list_comments, get_activity, clear_activity, search_issues. Inferred from message text when omitted.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Linear: create/get/update/delete issue, create/update/delete/list comment, search issues, get/clear activity.

exampleCalls.user: Use LINEAR with the provided parameters.

actions.name: LIST_ACTIVE_BLOCKS
actions.description: List the live website blocker status and any active managed website block rules, including their gate type and gate target. Only use this for website/app blocking status. Do not use it for inbox blockers, message priority, morning briefs, night briefs, operating pictures, end-of-day reviews, or general executive-assistant triage.

parameters.name: includeLiveStatus
parameters.description: Whether to include the current hosts-file/SelfControl live block state.

parameters.name: includeManagedRules
parameters.description: Whether to include managed LifeOps block rules and gate metadata.

actions.descriptionCompressed: List live website blocker status and active block rules.

exampleCalls.user: Use LIST_ACTIVE_BLOCKS with the provided parameters.

actions.name: LIST_AGENTS
actions.description: List active task agents together with current task progress so the main agent can keep the user updated while work continues asynchronously.

actions.name: LIST_LINEAR_COMMENTS
actions.description: List comments on a Linear issue

parameters.name: issueId
parameters.description: Linear issue id or identifier to list comments for.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of comments to return (default 25, max 100).

actions.descriptionCompressed: list comment Linear issue

exampleCalls.user: Use LIST_LINEAR_COMMENTS with the provided parameters.

actions.name: LIST_OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS
actions.description: List contacts whose last-contacted-at timestamp exceeds their follow-up threshold.

parameters.name: thresholdDays
parameters.description: Override the default overdue threshold in days for this query.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of overdue contacts to return.

exampleCalls.user: Use LIST_OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS with the provided parameters.

actions.name: lp_management
actions.description: Single LP management action. Params: subaction=onboard|list_pools|open|close|reposition|list_positions|get_position|set_preferences, chain=solana|evm, dex, pool, position, amount, range, tokenA, tokenB, chainId, slippageBps.

parameters.name: subaction
parameters.description: LP operation: onboard, list_pools, open, close, reposition, list_positions, get_position, set_preferences.

parameters.name: chain
parameters.description: Chain for the LP operation.

parameters.name: dex
parameters.description: DEX/protocol name.

parameters.name: pool
parameters.description: Pool id/address for open, close, reposition, or position lookup.

parameters.name: position
parameters.description: LP position id/mint/address.

parameters.name: amount
parameters.description: Liquidity amount for open, close, or reposition operations.

parameters.name: range
parameters.description: Desired concentrated liquidity price range.

parameters.name: tokenA
parameters.description: First token filter or deposit token.

parameters.name: tokenB
parameters.description: Second token filter or deposit token.

parameters.name: chainId
parameters.description: Optional numeric EVM chain id.

parameters.name: slippageBps
parameters.description: Maximum allowed slippage in basis points.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Manage LP positions by subaction, chain, dex, pool, position, amount, range, token filters.

exampleCalls.user: Use lp_management with the provided parameters.

actions.name: LS
actions.description: List entries in a directory, sorted with directories first then files. Each directory name has a trailing '/'. Pass an `ignore` array of glob patterns to skip entries. Use this instead of BASH for directory listing.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Absolute path of the directory to list. Defaults to the session cwd.

parameters.name: ignore
parameters.description: Array of glob patterns to exclude (e.g. ['*.log', 'tmp/*']).

actions.descriptionCompressed: List a directory; dirs first, files second; supports ignore globs.

exampleCalls.user: Use LS with the provided parameters.

actions.name: MANAGE_ISSUES
actions.description: Manage GitHub issues for a repository.

parameters.name: operation
parameters.description: The operation to perform: create, list, get, update, comment, close, reopen, add_labels
parameters.descriptionCompressed: Manage GitHub issues: create, list, comment, close, reopen.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: Repository in owner/repo format or full GitHub URL.

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: Issue title (for create operation).

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: Issue body/description (for create or comment operations).

parameters.name: issueNumber
parameters.description: Issue number (for get, update, comment, close, reopen operations).

parameters.name: labels
parameters.description: Labels to add (comma-separated string or array).

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Filter by state: open, closed, or all (for list operation).

actions.descriptionCompressed: manage GitHub issue repository support create issue, list issue, get issue detail, add comment, updat, close, reopen issue

exampleCalls.user: Use MANAGE_ISSUES with the provided parameters.

actions.name: MANAGE_WINDOW
actions.description:
manage_window_action:
  purpose: Manage desktop windows: list visible windows, focus or switch, arrange or move, minimize, maximize, restore, and close.
  guidance: Use list first to discover window IDs, then use focused window actions.
  actions: list/focus/switch/arrange/move/minimize/maximize/restore/close.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Window action to perform.

parameters.name: windowId
parameters.description: Window identifier.

parameters.name: windowTitle
parameters.description: Window title or app-name query for switch/restore/focus operations.

parameters.name: arrangement
parameters.description: Layout for arrange: tile, cascade, vertical, or horizontal.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: Target X coordinate for move.

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: Target Y coordinate for move.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Window management router: list/focus/switch/arrange/move/minimize/maximize/restore/close; list first to discover window ids.

exampleCalls.user: Use MANAGE_WINDOW with the provided parameters.

actions.name: N8N
actions.description: Manage n8n workflows. Operations: create (build new), modify (edit existing), activate (enable), deactivate (disable), delete (with confirmation), executions (get run history).

parameters.name: op
parameters.description: Operation: create, modify, activate, deactivate, delete, or executions. If omitted, inferred from message text.

parameters.name: workflowId
parameters.description: Exact n8n workflow id. When omitted, the workflow is matched semantically.

parameters.name: workflowName
parameters.description: Workflow name fragment for fuzzy matching (executions only).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max executions to return (executions only). Default 10.

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: Natural-language description (create/modify only). If omitted, derived from message.

actions.name: NOTEBOOK_EDIT
actions.description: Replace, insert, or delete a cell in a Jupyter `.ipynb` notebook. Default `edit_mode` is `replace`. Insert places a new cell after `cell_id` (or at the start if omitted). Delete removes the matching cell. The notebook must have been READ in this session and must still match its recorded mtime.

parameters.name: notebook_path
parameters.description: Absolute path to a .ipynb notebook.

parameters.name: cell_id
parameters.description: Target cell id. Required for replace and delete; optional for insert.

parameters.name: new_source
parameters.description: New cell source text. Required for replace and insert.

parameters.name: cell_type
parameters.description: Cell type: code | markdown | raw.

parameters.name: edit_mode
parameters.description: replace | insert | delete (default replace).

actions.descriptionCompressed: Replace/insert/delete a cell in a Jupyter notebook by cell_id.

exampleCalls.user: Use NOTEBOOK_EDIT with the provided parameters.

actions.name: PLAY_MUSIC_QUERY
actions.description: Handle any complex music query that requires understanding and research, then queue the selected track after confirmed:true. Supports: artist queries (first single, latest song, similar artists, popular songs, nth album), temporal (80s, 90s, specific years), genre/mood/vibe, activities (workout, study, party), charts/trending, albums, movie/game/TV soundtracks, lyrics/topics, versions (covers, remixes, acoustic, live), and more. Uses Wikipedia, music databases, and web search to find the right music.

parameters.name: confirmed
parameters.description: Must be true to resolve the music query and add the result to the queue.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Complex music search: artist, genre, mood, era, activity, charts, soundtracks, versions. Uses web search + databases.

exampleCalls.user: Use PLAY_MUSIC_QUERY with the provided parameters.

parameters.description: Number of days after last contact before this contact is considered overdue.

actions.name: SHOPIFY
actions.description: Manage a Shopify store. Operations: products (CRUD on products), inventory (stock adjustments), orders (list/update orders), customers (CRUD on customers). Op is inferred from the message text when not explicitly provided. For read-only catalog browsing use SEARCH_SHOPIFY_STORE.

parameters.name: op
parameters.description: Operation to perform. One of: products, inventory, orders, customers. Inferred from message text when omitted.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Shopify: products, inventory, orders, customers.

actions.name: SKILL
actions.description: Manage skill catalog. Operations: search (browse available skills), details (info about a specific skill), sync (refresh catalog from registry), toggle (enable/disable installed skill), install (install from registry), uninstall (remove non-bundled skill). For invoking an enabled skill, use USE_SKILL instead.

parameters.name: op
parameters.description: Operation to perform. One of: search, details, sync, toggle, install, uninstall. Inferred from message text when omitted.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Skill catalog: search, details, sync, toggle, install, uninstall.

exampleCalls.user: Use SKILL with the provided parameters.

actions.name: SUBMIT_WORKSPACE

parameters.name: skipPR

actions.name: TASK_CONTROL
actions.description: Pause, stop, resume, continue, archive, or reopen a coordinator task thread while preserving the durable thread history.

parameters.name: operation
parameters.description: Control operation to apply to the task thread.

parameters.name: threadId
parameters.description: Specific task thread id to control.

parameters.name: sessionId
parameters.description: Task session id to resolve into a thread when needed.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search text used to find the relevant thread.
parameters.descriptionCompressed: Search text for finding relevant thread.

parameters.name: note
parameters.description: Optional reason for pausing or stopping the thread.

parameters.name: instruction
parameters.description: Follow-up instruction for resume or continue operations.

parameters.name: agentType
parameters.description: Optional framework override for a resumed task.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Pause/stop/resume/archive/reopen coordinator task thread.

exampleCalls.user: Use TASK_CONTROL with the provided parameters.

actions.name: TASK_HISTORY
actions.description: Query coordinator task history without stuffing raw transcripts into model context. Use this for active work, yesterday/last-week summaries, topic search, counts, and thread detail lookup.

parameters.name: metric
parameters.description: Query mode: list, count, or detail.

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: Relative time window for the query.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Topic or free-text search string to match task threads.

parameters.name: statuses
parameters.description: Optional status filter list.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of thread summaries to return.

parameters.name: includeArchived
parameters.description: Whether archived threads should be included.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Query task history: active work, summaries, search, thread details.

exampleCalls.user: Use TASK_HISTORY with the provided parameters.

actions.name: TASK_OUTPUT
actions.description: Read captured output and current status of a background BASH task. Pass block=true to wait for completion (or until timeout) before returning.

parameters.name: task_id
parameters.description: Task id returned by BASH (background or auto-promoted).

parameters.name: block
parameters.description: If true, wait for the task to finish (or until timeout) before returning.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: When blocking, max ms to wait. Clamped to [0, 600000]. Default 30000 when block=true, 0 otherwise.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Read background shell task output (optionally blocking).

exampleCalls.user: Use TASK_OUTPUT with the provided parameters.

parameters.description: Specific task thread id to inspect.

parameters.name: sessionId

parameters.name: search

parameters.descriptionCompressed: Search text for finding task thread to share.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Find best way to view/share task result: artifacts, live URLs, paths.

exampleCalls.user: Use TASK_SHARE with the provided parameters.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Stop a background shell task.

actions.name: TODO_WRITE
actions.description: Replace the conversation's todo list with the provided array. Each todo has content, status (pending|in_progress|completed), and an optional activeForm describing the in-progress phrasing. The full list is replaced on every call. Use to plan multi-step work and track progress within a session.

parameters.name: todos
parameters.description: Array of todo objects. Each item: { id?: string, content: string, status: 'pending'|'in_progress'|'completed', activeForm?: string }. Replaces the entire list.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Replace conversation todo list with {content,status,activeForm}[].

exampleCalls.user: Use TODO_WRITE with the provided parameters.

actions.name: USE_SKILL
actions.description: Invoke an enabled skill by slug. The skill's instructions or script run and the result returns to the conversation.

actions.name: WALLET_PREPARE
actions.description: Prepare a non-binding wallet proposal. Set kind="swap" to fetch a BSC

parameters.name: kind
parameters.description: Proposal kind: "swap" for a BSC swap quote, "transfer" for a token transfer preview.

parameters.name: fromSymbol
parameters.description: Source asset symbol (swap only, e.g. "BNB", "USDT"). One of fromSymbol/toSymbol must be BNB on BSC.

parameters.name: toSymbol
parameters.description: Destination asset symbol (swap only, e.g. "BNB", "USDT"). One of fromSymbol/toSymbol must be BNB on BSC.

parameters.name: fromAddress
parameters.description: Source token contract address (swap only — required when the source asset is not BNB).

parameters.name: slippageBps
parameters.description: Slippage tolerance in basis points (swap only, default 300 = 3%).

parameters.name: toAddress
parameters.description: Recipient EVM address (transfer) or destination token contract address (swap, required when destination is not BNB). 0x-prefixed, 40 hex characters.

parameters.name: assetSymbol
parameters.description: Token symbol to transfer (transfer only, e.g. "BNB", "USDT", "USDC").

parameters.name: amount
parameters.description: Human-readable amount. For swaps the source-asset units (e.g. "0.5"); for transfers the asset units (e.g. "1.5", "100").

actions.descriptionCompressed: Wallet preview ops: swap, transfer.

exampleCalls.user: Use WALLET_PREPARE with the provided parameters.

actions.name: WEB_FETCH
actions.description: Fetch a single http(s) URL and return its body as text. HTML responses are stripped of tags and collapsed to plain text. Body is capped at 5MB; text is capped at 50000 chars. Loopback addresses (localhost, 127.0.0.1, 0.0.0.0, ::1, 169.254.*) are blocked by default; set CODING_TOOLS_WEB_FETCH_ALLOW_LOOPBACK=1 to permit them. Use for reading documentation, blog posts, or pasting in a URL the user referenced.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: Absolute http:// or https:// URL to fetch.

parameters.name: prompt
parameters.description: Optional summary/extraction instruction. Echoed verbatim in the result text; no LLM is run on the body in v1.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Fetch http(s) URL and return body as plain text (HTML stripped, capped).

exampleCalls.user: Use WEB_FETCH with the provided parameters.

actions.name: WORKFLOW
actions.description: n8n workflow lifecycle operation. Pass `op` ("activate", "deactivate", or "delete") and optionally `workflowId`. Identifies workflows by ID, name, or semantic description.

parameters.name: op
parameters.description: Lifecycle operation to perform. One of: activate, deactivate, delete.

parameters.name: workflowId
parameters.description: Exact n8n workflow id. When omitted, the workflow is matched semantically.

actions.descriptionCompressed: n8n workflow lifecycle: activate, deactivate, delete.

exampleCalls.user: Use WORKFLOW with the provided parameters.

actions.name: WRITE
actions.description: Write a file at an absolute path, replacing any existing contents. The file's parent directory is created if missing. Existing files must have been READ in this session first; otherwise the write is rejected to avoid clobbering external edits.

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.description: Absolute path to the file to write.

parameters.name: content
parameters.description: Full new file contents.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Write a file at an absolute path (creates parents; rejects if existing file was not READ first).

exampleCalls.user: Use WRITE with the provided parameters.
